skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X account actions through the inference.sh CLI, including posts, media posts, likes, retweets, DMs, follows, profile lookup, and post deletion.
skill.summary: Run inference.sh CLI workflows for Twitter/X posting and engagement tasks.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are mostly true positives because the skill intentionally instructs agents to run the external infsh and npx CLIs. No confirmed malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection was found, but the skill can perform high-impact Twitter/X account actions and should be published only with clear user authorization warnings.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Can Perform Account-Changing Twitter/X Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill authorizes Bash(infsh *) and provides commands that post tweets, create media posts, like posts, retweet, delete posts, send DMs, and follow users. These are legitimate stated capabilities, but they can change a connected social account and must require explicit user approval before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and examples directly show account-changing infsh commands. The risk is high-impact misuse rather than hidden malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Command Output Redirects Generated Media Metadata to Local Files
medium_findings.description: The image and video workflows redirect CLI output into image.json and video.json. This appears intentional, but outputs may contain generated media URLs or metadata that should be treated as sensitive until reviewed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The redirections are explicit and semantically relevant to posting media. The sensitivity depends on what the infsh apps return, which is not shown in this file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography findings at lines 3 and 27 do not show hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage. Line 3 is descriptive metadata and line 27 is an apps table header.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no crypto-related code or instructions. The scanner likely matched short tokens in natural language or table text.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Media URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh documentation and a hosted image, plus a placeholder media URL. These are expected for documentation, but they indicate network-dependent workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or example resources and do not include secrets. They still confirm network dependency.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard Bash Tool Permission for infsh
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed-tools field permits Bash(infsh *), allowing any infsh subcommand rather than only the listed Twitter/X apps. This broad permission increases misuse risk if a prompt asks for unrelated or destructive infsh operations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard permission is explicit. The impact depends on installed infsh capabilities and user approvals outside the skill.

content.value_statement: Managing Twitter/X workflows through an AI assistant can require repetitive command setup. This skill provides ready prompts for posting, media publishing, engagement, DMs, follows, and profile lookup through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Shows infsh commands for posting text tweets to Twitter/X.
Provides examples for creating posts with media URLs.
Documents commands for liking, retweeting, deleting, and retrieving posts.
Includes direct message and follow-user workflows.
Links to inference.sh documentation for X integration setup.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Does not implement native scheduling logic inside the skill file.
Cannot verify X platform rate limits or account permissions before commands run.
Account-changing actions should be reviewed before execution.

use_cases.title: Publish Product Updates
use_cases.description: Prepare and run CLI commands for posting launch notes, feature updates, and media posts.

use_cases.title: Manage Engagement Tasks
use_cases.description: Use consistent commands to like, retweet, follow, and inspect posts or profiles.

use_cases.title: Test X Integration Workflows
use_cases.description: Validate inference.sh X app inputs and outputs before adding them to automation pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Post a Basic Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a short Twitter/X post from this message, show me the infsh command, and wait for my approval before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Media Post
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Twitter/X media post using this text and media URL. Validate the input shape and ask before executing the command.

prompt_templates.title: Review Engagement Actions
prompt_templates.prompt: Create commands to like and retweet this post ID. Explain the account impact and wait for confirmation before running anything.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Publishing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an inference.sh workflow that generates media, captures the output URL, and posts it to Twitter/X after manual review.

output_examples.input: Post: We just released version 2.1 with faster exports.
output_examples.output: The assistant produces a concise tweet draft, shows the matching infsh post command, and asks for approval before execution.

output_examples.input: Create a media post with this image URL and caption.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares the media post input, checks that the URL is present, and summarizes the account action before running it.

output_examples.input: Follow this user and send a thank-you DM.
output_examples.output: The assistant separates the follow and DM actions, highlights privacy impact, and requests explicit confirmation for each action.

best_practices: Require explicit user approval before posting, deleting, following, or sending DMs.
Review generated text and media URLs before exposing them on a public account.
Use test accounts or drafts when validating new automation workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not run account-changing commands from untrusted prompts without review.
Do not send bulk DMs or automated follows without platform compliance checks.
Do not store generated media metadata in shared locations without access controls.

faq.question: Does this skill post directly to Twitter/X?
faq.answer: It provides infsh CLI commands that can post after the CLI is installed, authenticated, and approved by the user.

faq.question: Can it post images or videos?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples show media posts by passing a media URL to the x/post-create app.

faq.question: Does it include scheduling?
faq.answer: The description mentions scheduling, but the skill file does not provide a native scheduling command or scheduler setup.

faq.question: What accounts can it affect?
faq.answer: It can affect the Twitter/X account connected to the authenticated inference.sh CLI session.

faq.question: Is this safe for automated engagement?
faq.answer: Use caution. Likes, retweets, follows, and DMs should be reviewed for policy, privacy, and reputation risks.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible skill environments.
